it is odd and parasocial, but truthfully i've only seen it from a small handful of inviduals so i don't think it should be dignified with comments really, better for it just to be ignored as i also think the staff wouldn't take such statements very seriously either. 

i know it has infamously happened to a degree in some other gacha games, but it seems practically guaranteed that only an extremely miniscule percentage of the playerbase (even moreso the high-paying playerbase, probably) are vociferous ideological "no males", or for that matter ""no females"" types. though i don't have insight into every part of the community, of course.

...also, this game is really fecking gay. they'd be barking up the wrong tree, so to speak.

though on a personal level i do hope the next guy character feels like more of a mature adult who's lived a good amount of their (probably horrendous) life, castor's affection dialogues make me feel like a dodgy woman to be honest. <:sad:1356085505523187712>

Hi Keeper! Yes we just fixed it. I’m just leaving the workplace after overtime. I was lurking here and I’m so sorry the pronoun confusion and “senpai” had been there since eternity. <:please:1356053110375714817>… I noticed that people often had to double check with the Chinese version because of bad English mtl. While we still can’t fully improve the entire main story yet, we hope by updating the archives translation, we can help Keepers better understand the world of Morimens. If you notice any issues after monday’s update, please let me know!

even Karen's narrative as a whole reminds me of a lot of... especially Assyrian proverbs, mythologies and religious carvings. as a society, the Assyrians were (ironically, considering their general reputation, which was often based on propaganda - not that I'm saying they were without fault) extremely against and fearful of rampant greed that cannot be sated.

it's a lot like the "rabisu" myths, the oldest possible vampire-like mythologies known to exist - human-like beings who would progressively drain the hopes, will to exist and eventually the life energies of humans and other beings.

in any case, there were a lot of mythologies of an originally relatively weak, harmless or innocent being becoming consumed by insatiable [non-sexual] desires that others failed to notice and eventually spiralling into a rabisu or a  mass murderer
in a lovecraft-inspired work
